title: createSignal
description: Creates a reactive signal with a getter and setter.
signature:
createSignal<T>(): [Accessor<T | undefined>, Setter<T | undefined>]
createSignal<T>(value: T, options?: SignalOptions<T>): [Accessor<T>, Setter<T>]
param_value_desc: Initial value of the signal. Optional; omit for <0>T | undefined</0>.
param_options_desc: Optional. Set <0>equals</0> to <1>false</1> to disable equality checks, or provide a custom comparator.
returns_getter: <0>getter()</0>: reads the current value and registers a dependency when called inside a tracking scope
returns_setter: <0>setter(value)</0>: updates the value. Accepts a direct value or a function <1>(prev) => next</1>.
usage_sweep_state: The uptime monitor's <0>App</0> holds every probe sample and per-check tally in signals. Each write uses the functional form <1>prev => next</1>, so a new sample appends to that target's history without touching the rest.
usage_latest_probe: Each <0>HttpCheck</0> keeps its most recent probe result in a signal. The sweep loop writes it through <1>setLatest</1>, and every memo or effect that reads <2>latest()</2> re-runs when the next sweep lands.
tip_equality: By default, signals use <0>===</0> comparison. Setting the same value again won't trigger re-computation.
in_the_wild: See these signals drive a live status page in the <0>uptime-monitor walkthrough</0>.
